ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "operator_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol representing the operator (e.g., &, |, ^, ~, <<) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the operator (e.g., and, or, xor, not, left shift) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operator and its function */,
  "usage_instructions" TEXT /* Detailed instructions on how to use the operator */,
  "examples_link" TEXT /* Link to a detailed page with more examples and use cases */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the operator belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operators_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES operator_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "examples" (
  "example_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each example */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator the example belongs to */,
  "input_example" TEXT /* Example input for the operator */,
  "result" INTEGER /* Result of the operation */,
  "example_description" TEXT /* Description of the example and its significance */,
  "example_category" TEXT /* Category of the example (e.g., basic, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("example_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_examples_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator used in the operation */,
  "input_a" INTEGER /* First input operand */,
  "input_b" INTEGER /* Second input operand (if applicable) */,
  "result" INTEGER /* Result of the operation */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the operation was performed */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Time taken to execute the operation */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who performed the operation */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* ID of the user's session for tracking */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, engineer, admin) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User's account status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_operations" (
  "user_operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user operation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the operation was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user when performing the operation */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* ID of the user's session for tracking */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_operations_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_operations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator history */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator */,
  "usage_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the operator has been used */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last usage */,
  "total_execution_time" REAL /* Total time taken for all operations using the operator */,
  "average_execution_time" REAL /* Average time taken for operations using the operator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_history_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operation_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided the feedback */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation the feedback is about */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the feedback was provided */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1 to 5 stars) */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the feedback has been addressed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operation_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operation_feedback_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for nested categories) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_to_category" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the operator belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_to_category_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES operator_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_to_category_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operation_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation */,
  "input_a" INTEGER /* First input operand */,
  "input_b" INTEGER /* Second input operand (if applicable) */,
  "result" INTEGER /* Result of the operation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator used in the operation */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the operation was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the operation (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Time taken to execute the operation */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who performed the operation */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* ID of the user's session for tracking */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operation_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operation_logs_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operation_logs_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator */,
  "usage_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the user has used the operator */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last usage */,
  "total_execution_time" REAL /* Total time taken for all operations using the operator by the user */,
  "average_execution_time" REAL /* Average time taken for operations using the operator by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_usage_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_usage_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided the feedback */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the feedback was provided */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1 to 5 stars) */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the feedback has been addressed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
